title: Signet: D-Bus Credentials interface leaks credentials and mints fake sessions
description: dbus_common.c credential handlers do not enforce per-agent scoping and do not perform real credential exchange. (1) GetToken (dbus_common.c:396-426) looks up a secret by credential TYPE only, ignoring the caller's agent_id, then returns the DECRYPTED payload hex-encoded to the caller. Any authenticated D-Bus/TCP client can retrieve any credential of a given type regardless of ownership. (2) GetSession (dbus_common.c:346-393) generates a random 32-byte token locally and returns it with a 24h expiry; it never exchanges the stored credential with a service (no session_broker/HTTP). In store-less mode it returns an untracked opaque token; with a store it records a lease keyed by token hash but the token is still just random bytes. README claims GetSession 'exchanges a stored credential for a service session token'.
acceptance_criteria: 1) GetToken requires and enforces agent_id ownership of the credential; unauthorized access is denied. 2) GetToken returns a leased/brokered token, not the raw stored secret (or is explicitly documented as returning the credential with tight authz). 3) GetSession performs real credential exchange via session_broker (or fails closed when no service configured) rather than minting a random bearer token. 4) Tests cover cross-agent denial and exchange behavior.
notes: Evidence: dbus_common.c:346 randombytes_buf token; :358 store-less opaque token; :396-426 GetToken by type, no agent_id, returns payload hex. Also D-Bus TCP shares this dispatch.

title: Signet: revoke_agent bypasses deny list, lease revocation, and revocation audit
description: Management REVOKE_AGENT handler (mgmt_protocol.c:475) calls only signet_key_store_revoke_agent(h->keys, agent_id), which wipes the key from cache/store. It does NOT call the full revocation path in revocation.c (signet_revoke_agent), so the agent pubkey is NEVER added to the deny list, active leases are NOT revoked, and no revocation audit entry is written. README states revoke 'adds to deny list' and 'deny list takes precedence over all other authorization'. Consequence: a revoked agent that already holds a live NIP-46 session/lease, or that gets re-provisioned, is not actually denied.
acceptance_criteria: 1) REVOKE_AGENT adds the agent pubkey to the SQLCipher deny list. 2) Active leases/sessions for the agent are revoked. 3) A revocation audit entry is recorded. 4) After revoke, any subsequent request from that pubkey is denied by deny-list precedence even if a session/policy would allow it. 5) Test proves deny-list precedence post-revoke.
notes: Evidence: mgmt_protocol.c:475 signet_key_store_revoke_agent only; revocation.c signet_revoke_agent unused by mgmt path.
close_reason: Implemented: mgmt REVOKE_AGENT now resolves the agent pubkey and calls signet_revoke_agent (deny-list add + lease burn + key wipe + audit) via the shared live deny list wired through signet_mgmt_handler_set_deny_list in signetd_main. Falls back to cache-only key wipe when no persistent store. Builds clean; deny-precedence e2e test tracked by nostrc-4dp.

title: Signet: bootstrap tokens are not single-use (consume never called; replayable)
description: POST /bootstrap (bootstrap_server.c handle_auth) verifies a token via signet_store_verify_bootstrap_token() (line ~204) and then binds a handoff secret via signet_store_bind_bootstrap_token_handoff() (line ~283), but NEVER calls signet_store_consume_bootstrap_token(). verify only increments attempt_count; it does not set used_at. So a valid token can be redeemed repeatedly until it hits max_attempts or expiry, contradicting the README 'single-use bootstrap tokens' guarantee. The consume function exists but is dead code on this path. Token verify+attempt-increment is also split across two non-atomic SQL statements (store_tokens.c:44 UPDATE then :61 SELECT), racy under concurrency, and lookup uses ordinary SQL equality (not constant-time).
acceptance_criteria: 1) A token that successfully mints a bunker URI is marked used (used_at set) atomically and cannot be redeemed again (returns ALREADY_USED). 2) verify+consume is a single transaction or otherwise race-safe. 3) Test: redeem once succeeds, second redeem of same token fails with ALREADY_USED.
notes: Evidence: bootstrap_server.c:204 verify, :283 bind_handoff, no consume; store_tokens.c:44,61,91 (used_at never set on verify); signet_store_consume_bootstrap_token exists but unused on /bootstrap.
close_reason: Implemented: POST /bootstrap now calls signet_store_consume_bootstrap_token after minting the URI, atomically marking the token used (UPDATE ... WHERE used_at IS NULL). Concurrent/replayed redemptions are rejected with 403 token already used. Builds clean; single-use regression test tracked by nostrc-4dp.

title: Signet: SQLCipher at-rest encryption is not enforced (silent plaintext DB)
description: store.c opens the DB with plain sqlite3_open() then runs PRAGMA key, but if PRAGMA key fails (regular SQLite, not SQLCipher) the error is swallowed: store.c:255-259 comment 'If this is regular SQLite ... We continue'. CMakeLists.txt:40,131 links PkgConfig::SQLITE3 (plain sqlite3), NOT sqlcipher. Result: in default builds the DB is unencrypted at rest. There is no PRAGMA cipher_version verification and no keyed-read validation. README claims 'AES-256 encrypted SQLite database'. Worse, agents.connect_secret and bootstrap token/handoff secrets are stored as plaintext TEXT (store.c:117), so with no SQLCipher these secrets sit in cleartext on disk. Envelope encryption only covers encrypted_nsec and secret payloads, not connect secrets.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Build links SQLCipher (or fails loudly if unavailable when encryption is required). 2) On open, verify SQLCipher is active (PRAGMA cipher_version non-empty AND a keyed read of sqlite_master succeeds); refuse to start (or require explicit opt-in) if the DB is unencrypted. 3) connect_secret and bootstrap handoff secrets are either envelope-encrypted or documented+gated. 4) Test proves DB bytes on disk contain no plaintext connect_secret and that a wrong key fails to open.
notes: Evidence: store.c:236 sqlite3_open; store.c:251-259 PRAGMA key failure ignored; CMakeLists.txt:40,131-132; store.c:117 connect_secret TEXT plaintext.

title: Signet production-readiness: eliminate fake/incomplete security behavior
description: Umbrella epic tracking a deep app-wide review of the signet NIP-46 bunker (2026-07-03). A read-only audit across all subsystems (sign path, storage/crypto, mgmt protocol, auth/bootstrap, transports, config, tests) found multiple places where the code reports success without performing its stated security work, with no TODO/marker comments. Child issues capture each defect with file:line evidence. Priorities: P0 = at-rest encryption + authz/replay/revocation correctness; P1 = fail-open behavior, transport auth, delivery integrity, fake tests; P2 = doc/impl mismatches and hardening.
notes: Children (18 issues): P0 nostrc-1ww (SQLCipher), nostrc-ufh (bootstrap single-use), nostrc-sd3 (revoke deny-list), nostrc-8q2 (D-Bus cred leak/fake session). P1 nostrc-593 (relay publish false success), nostrc-aoe (mgmt plaintext ACK), nostrc-e5y (session_broker fake token), nostrc-t04 (SO_PEERCRED unwired), nostrc-zi6 (ssh wrong-identity), nostrc-lt1 (no SIGHUP reload), nostrc-hx9 (missing nip44 handlers), nostrc-o02 (relay recv no verify), nostrc-5tc (rotation non-txn), nostrc-dhd (fake rate/auth tests), nostrc-23g (no at-rest/sign/nip44 tests). P2 nostrc-d75 (set_policy best-effort), nostrc-fao (replay fail-open), nostrc-9np (DB key/KDF docs), nostrc-op9 (single DEK), nostrc-p0s (wipe/audit-chain/policy-default grab-bag), nostrc-aff (FIDO half-wired), nostrc-a6g (FIDO test shims/CI gating), nostrc-4dp (missing e2e tests). Audit doc: this session 2026-07-03.

title: [F02] Fix nip42_Event struct missing sig field — broken NIP-42 validation
description: CRITICAL: nip42_Event struct in nip42.h lacks a sig member, while nip42_validate_auth_event() calls nostr_check_signature(event->id, event->pubkey, event->sig). This either fails to compile or reads garbage memory. Add char* sig to the struct and populate it in nip42_create_unsigned_auth_event. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F02
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: [F01] Fix relay_optimized.c dropping OK/CLOSED/AUTH/COUNT frames
description: CRITICAL: relay_optimized.c process_envelope() silently drops all non-EVENT control frames (OK, CLOSED, AUTH, COUNT, NOTICE). Route all frame types through a shared dispatch function used by both relay.c and relay_optimized.c. The optimized path should only optimize parsing/allocation, not skip protocol semantics. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F01
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: CRITICAL: hanami_clone is fundamentally broken
description: Three compounding bugs: (1) Passes Blossom endpoint URL to git_clone — not a git remote. (2) Returns temp bare repo instead of local clone to the caller. (3) Temp directory from mkdtemp is never cleaned up. File: libhanami/src/hanami.c lines 213-338.
close_reason: Fixed by parallel agent dispatch

title: CRITICAL: hanami_push_to_blossom only uploads commit objects
description: hanami_push_to_blossom() uses git_revwalk which only yields commit OIDs. Tree and blob objects are never uploaded, making pushed repos completely unusable. The revwalk must be supplemented with recursive tree walking to enumerate and upload all reachable trees and blobs. File: libhanami/src/hanami.c lines 395-422.
close_reason: Replaced git_revwalk with git_odb_foreach in hanami_push_to_blossom to enumerate all object types (commits, trees, blobs, tags). Extracted callback to file-scope static function for C11 compliance.

title: Signet TESTS: no proof of at-rest encryption, signing correctness, or NIP-44 round-trip
description: Core security properties are untested. (1) test_store.c:388-396 only checks signet_store_get_dek()!=NULL; it does NOT verify SQLCipher/at-rest encryption, that the DB is unreadable without the key, or wrong-key rejection. (2) test_key_store.c:46-70 loads secret bytes and pubkey but never signs a real Nostr event or verifies the Schnorr signature/pubkey against NIP-01. (3) There is no NIP-44 encrypt/decrypt round-trip test across two identities (tamper/wrong-key rejection). (4) test_signet_smoke.c:20-83 constructs objects and returns 0 without asserting any of them are non-NULL or that start/teardown succeeded.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Store test: DB file bytes unreadable/plaintext-free without key; wrong key fails to open (requires SQLCipher issue fixed). 2) Signing test: provision/load agent key, sign canonical event JSON, independently verify id+pubkey+Schnorr sig. 3) NIP-44 round-trip + tamper/wrong-peer rejection test. 4) Smoke test asserts object creation and clean teardown.
notes: Evidence: test_store.c:388-396; test_key_store.c:46-70; test_signet_smoke.c:20-83; no nip44 round-trip test present.

title: Signet TESTS: rate-limit and auth tests do not prove the behavior they name
description: Two test files assert nothing meaningful about the security property under test. (1) test_capability.c:259-286 is named for rate limiting but calls signet_policy_rate_limit_check() in a loop and never asserts the limit is ENFORCED after the threshold — it would pass if the limiter always returned allow. (2) test_nostr_auth.c:78-95 only checks that invalid/null JSON is rejected; it NEVER verifies a valid signed Nostr auth event succeeds, nor checks challenge binding, wrong-challenge rejection, expiry, replay, or pubkey mismatch. Both are placeholder-grade and give false confidence.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Rate-limit test asserts: first N allowed, N+1 denied, and window reset restores allowance. 2) Auth test constructs a real signed kind-28100 challenge response and asserts SIGNET_AUTH_OK, plus explicit failures for wrong/expired/replayed challenge and pubkey mismatch. 3) Tests fail if the implementation is replaced with an always-allow/always-ok stub.
notes: PARTIAL: rate-limit half DONE — test_capability.c test_rate_limit rewritten to assert real enforcement (first admitted, 2nd + 20-burst denied at 5/hour where burst clamps to 1), per-capability bucket isolation, unlimited (0/hour) pass-through, and burst-scaling at 36000/hour. Also WIRED test_capability into meson (was cmake-only, never run by primary build); now 6/6 meson tests pass. REMAINING: test_nostr_auth valid-signed-challenge verification (construct a real kind-28100 signed event, assert SIGNET_AUTH_OK, plus wrong/expired/replayed challenge + pubkey-mismatch failures).

title: Signet: credential rotation is non-transactional and archives versions best-effort
description: store_secrets.c rotation (store_secrets.c:265-311) INSERTs the old version into secret_versions then UPDATEs secrets.payload, but the archive INSERT prepare/step errors are ignored (best-effort) and the two statements are not wrapped in a transaction. A failure or crash between archive and update, or an ignored archive failure followed by a successful update, silently breaks version history while rotation reports success. README claims 'rotation archives the previous version'.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Archive+update run in a single transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT, ROLLBACK on any error). 2) Archive failure aborts rotation with an error. 3) Test: after rotation, secret_versions contains the prior version and the active payload is updated; simulated archive failure rolls back.
notes: Evidence: store_secrets.c:265-281 ignored step errors; :265-311 no transaction.
close_reason: Implemented and built; see commit. Details in each issue's fix.

title: Signet: relay receive helper dispatches events without Schnorr verification
description: signet_relay_pool_handle_event_json() (relay_pool.c:740-791) parses arbitrary event JSON and calls rp->on_event(...) WITHOUT verifying the event signature, unlike the live subscription middleware (relay_pool.c:82-89) which does verify. If this helper is used to feed events into the NIP-46 handling path, forged/unsigned events could be processed as authentic. Confirm all production receive paths verify signatures before dispatch.
acceptance_criteria: 1) handle_event_json verifies the Schnorr signature (and id) before dispatching, or is removed if unused. 2) A forged event (bad sig) is rejected. 3) Test covers rejection of tampered event.
notes: Evidence: relay_pool.c:740-791 no verify vs :82-89 verify in live path.
close_reason: Implemented and built; see commit.

title: Signet: NIP-46 dispatch missing nip44_encrypt/nip44_decrypt handlers
description: nip46_server.c method dispatch implements sign_event, nip04_encrypt, nip04_decrypt, connect, ping, get_public_key, get_relays and webauthn_* but has NO handler for nip44_encrypt/nip44_decrypt. These fall through to 'unsupported_method' (nip46_server.c:699-702). NIP-44 encryption is a core capability advertised in the README and is the modern encryption standard; NIP-46 clients requesting nip44_encrypt/nip44_decrypt will be rejected.
acceptance_criteria: 1) nip44_encrypt/nip44_decrypt handlers implemented, delegating to libnostr nostr_nip44_* with the agent key. 2) Policy/capability gating applies. 3) Round-trip test through the server path (encrypt then decrypt) passes.
notes: Evidence: nip46_server.c:544 dispatch chain only nip04_*; :699-702 unsupported fallthrough.
close_reason: Implemented and built; see commit. Details in each issue's fix.

title: Signet: no SIGHUP handler in signetd — runtime policy/config reload does not work
description: signetd installs only SIGINT and SIGTERM handlers (signetd_main.c:464-469) and loads config once (signetd_main.c:473-475). There is no SIGHUP handler despite the file header (signetd_main.c:13-14) and README claiming SIGHUP triggers policy-file reload without restart. Sending SIGHUP to signetd has no effect (default action may even terminate the process).
acceptance_criteria: 1) SIGHUP is handled and triggers a safe reload of the policy store (and any other reloadable config). 2) Reload is atomic and does not drop in-flight requests. 3) Test/integration: edit policy file, send SIGHUP, observe new policy takes effect without restart.
notes: Evidence: signetd_main.c:464-469 only SIGINT/SIGTERM; :473-475 one-time load; header:13-14 claims reload.
close_reason: INVALID as filed / already implemented. SIGHUP-driven policy reload IS wired: signetd calls signet_policy_store_file_new (signetd_main.c:610), which self-installs a SIGHUP handler (policy_store.c:1018) that sets g_signet_policy_sighup_requested and requests audit-log reopen (policy_store.c:85). On the request path signet_policy_engine_eval -> signet_policy_store_get -> signet_policy_store_maybe_reload_locked (policy_store.c:651,593) consumes the flag and reloads the policy file. The signetd header comment is accurate: reload is intentionally handled in the policy module, not signetd_main. Verified by tracing the eval path.

title: Signet: SSH agent ignores requested key blob and lacks ECDSA (wrong-identity signing)
description: ssh_agent.c handling of SSH_AGENTC_SIGN_REQUEST skips the requested key blob (ssh_agent.c:199-204 'dp += kb_len; /* skip key blob */') and signs with the mapped agent's key regardless of which identity the client asked for. If an agent has (or is expected to present) multiple identities, the wrong key can sign. Additionally only ed25519 is implemented (ssh_agent.c:225-258); ECDSA identities are not supported and all identities are hardcoded to ssh-ed25519, so listed keys may not match what actually signs.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Sign request matches the requested key blob against the agent's available identities and signs with the correct key, or rejects if no match. 2) Identity list reflects the real key type(s). 3) ECDSA either implemented or explicitly unsupported/omitted from the identity list. 4) Test: sign request for a non-owned/unknown blob is rejected.
notes: Evidence: ssh_agent.c:199-204 skip key blob; :225-258 ed25519-only.

title: Signet: SO_PEERCRED UID-to-agent auth is unwired in production (no uid_resolver)
description: signetd_main.c wires the D-Bus Unix transport (signetd_main.c:777-779) and SSH agent (signetd_main.c:857-859) with .uid_resolver = NULL / .uid_resolver_data = NULL. The dispatch layer's signet_dbus_resolve_sender() therefore always fails, meaning the advertised SO_PEERCRED UID->agent mapping is non-functional in the production build. Either these transports cannot authenticate callers to an agent, or they fall back to some default — either way the README's 'auth via SO_PEERCRED UID-to-agent mapping' is not delivered.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A real uid_resolver is wired in production config that maps peer UID -> agent_id from configuration/store. 2) Requests from an unmapped UID are denied. 3) SSH agent uses the same mapping. 4) Test/integration proves a mapped UID signs and an unmapped UID is rejected.
notes: Evidence: signetd_main.c:777-779, 857-859 uid_resolver=NULL; dbus_common signet_dbus_resolve_sender depends on it.

title: Signet: session_broker returns fake local token on exchange failure and ignores lease persistence errors
description: session_broker.c GetSession falls back to a locally generated random token when the HTTP credential exchange fails, no service URL is provided, or curl is unavailable (session_broker.c:235 'if (!exchanged) generate_local_token(...)'). Callers cannot distinguish a real brokered service session from a meaningless random token. It also ignores lease persistence failure ((void)signet_store_issue_lease at :263), so it can return success without recording lease state. There is no HTTPS scheme enforcement (comment at :137 claims it but no check), so credentials may be POSTed to plaintext HTTP.
acceptance_criteria: 1) On exchange failure GetSession fails closed (error), not a fake token — or the fallback is a clearly-typed Signet-issued session with tracked lease, never silently substituted for a service token. 2) Lease persistence failure is surfaced as an error. 3) service_url must be https (reject http) unless explicitly allowed for localhost. 4) Tests cover failure and http-rejection.
notes: Evidence: session_broker.c:235 fallback, :263 ignored lease error, :137 unenforced HTTPS comment.
close_reason: Implemented and built; see commit.

title: Signet: management ACKs fall back to unencrypted plaintext when NIP-44 encryption fails
description: The mgmt ACK builder (mgmt_protocol.c:359) does nostr_event_set_content(evt, encrypted_content ? encrypted_content : ack_content) — if NIP-44 encryption of the ack fails, it silently publishes the ACK content in PLAINTEXT rather than failing closed. Management ACKs can contain provisioning results (agent_id, pubkey, bunker_uri). This is a fail-open information leak that contradicts the 'management events are NIP-44 encrypted' guarantee. Related: get_status/list_agents requests with empty content bypass NIP-44 decryption entirely (mgmt_protocol.c:108), accepting unencrypted management requests.
acceptance_criteria: 1) If ack encryption fails, the ACK is NOT published in plaintext; the handler fails closed and logs an error. 2) Management requests are required to be NIP-44 encrypted (including get_status/list_agents) or the exception is explicitly justified and authorized. 3) Test: forced encryption failure produces no plaintext ACK on the wire.
notes: Evidence: mgmt_protocol.c:359 plaintext fallback; :108 empty-content bypasses decryption.
close_reason: Implemented: signet_mgmt_publish_ack now fails closed — if NIP-44 encryption of the ACK fails or no recipient resolves, it logs a warning and refuses to publish, never falling back to plaintext. Builds clean and all signet tests pass.

title: Signet: relay publish reports success even when zero relays are connected
description: signet_relay_pool_publish_event_json() (relay_pool.c:611-644) and signet_relay_pool_publish_event_json_ack() (relay_pool.c:685-735) loop over relays, publish only to connected ones, and unconditionally return 0. If no relay is connected (or all skipped), they still return 0 = success. The NIP-46 sign path (nip46_server.c:~770) treats return 0 as published=true, so a signed response can be reported delivered when nothing was actually sent. Same silent-success issue affects start/subscribe (relay_pool.c:475-488, 541-608): they return 0 without confirming any connection/subscription.
acceptance_criteria: 1) publish returns a distinct non-success code when no relay accepted the event (0 relays connected / 0 sends). 2) The NIP-46 path distinguishes enqueued-and-sent from dropped and audits/metrics accordingly. 3) Optionally track per-event OK acks to confirm at least one relay accepted. 4) Test with a pool of 0 connected relays asserts publish does not report success.
notes: Evidence: relay_pool.c:611-644, 685-735 return 0 regardless of send count; nip46_server.c published=(...==0).
close_reason: Implemented and built; see commit. Details in each issue's fix.

(F09) relayd auth challenge uses time(NULL) instead of existing gen_nonce() — predictable nonces enable replay attacks. (F10) nostr_relay_store.c casts GObject interface to C vtable struct — undefined behavior from type-punning incompatible layouts. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F08, F09, F10

(2) All passkey/FIDO tests are gated behind SIGNET_ENABLE_PASSKEYS (CMakeLists.txt:93-190, meson.build:51-97) which is OFF by default, so they are absent from normal CI. (3) The only live daemon+D-Bus interop harness is explicitly DISABLED (CMakeLists.txt:195-200 DISABLED TRUE) and even the manual script relies on test hooks that skip relay/auth (interop/run_live_dbus_interop.sh:170-177). title: Signet: FIDO2/passkeys authenticator is half-wired (signCount=0, UV rejected, ECDSA/only ES256, no origin check)
description: Experimental passkeys (SIGNET_ENABLE_PASSKEYS, off by default). Confirmed gaps that would prevent a real WebAuthn authenticator from working: (1) getAssertion always emits signCount 0 and never increments/persists a counter (fido.c:500-504; store_passkeys.h:31-32) — acceptable only for synced creds, breaks non-synced counter semantics. (2) CTAP-HID rejects uv=true before calling the FIDO service (fido_ctaphid.c:273-277) even when service config allow_headless_uv is set, so UV-requiring clients fail. (3) RP check is plain string equality on stored rp_id (fido.c:407-438); no clientDataHash/origin validation layer. (4) bootstrap_delivery/JSON escaping and AAGUID placeholder noted elsewhere. Enabling SIGNET_ENABLE_PASSKEYS today yields a non-conformant authenticator.
acceptance_criteria: 1) signCount policy documented + implemented (persist/increment for non-synced). 2) allow_headless_uv honored end-to-end through CTAP-HID. 3) Origin/RP policy validation defined and enforced or explicitly delegated with docs. 4) Passkey tests run in CI (see separate test issue).
notes: Evidence: fido.c:500-504,407-438; store_passkeys.h:31-32; fido_ctaphid.c:273-277.

title: Signet: secrets/connect-secrets not securely wiped; JSONL audit not hash-chained; policy default ignored
description: Grab-bag of confirmed hardening gaps. (1) connect_secret wiped with plain memset (store.c:520) which the compiler may elide; use sodium_memzero/explicit_bzero. (2) Encrypted secret-key and credential ciphertext buffers freed without secure wipe (store.c:360-362; store_secrets.c:92-94,309-311). (3) JSONL/file audit logger (audit_logger.c:150-210) writes plain records with NO prev_hash/entry_hash chaining — only the SQLite audit store (store_audit.c) is hash-chained, so file audit output is not tamper-evident as README implies. (4) policy_engine.c stores cfg->default_decision (policy_engine.c:27) but never uses it — missing identity/no-policy always hard-denies (:67-72); configured default is dead. (5) rotate_key only checks the hot cache (key_store.c:386) and returns not_found for agents that exist only in the store.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All secret/ciphertext buffers wiped with sodium_memzero before free. 2) File audit either hash-chained or README corrected to scope chaining to the SQLite store. 3) policy_engine honors configured default_decision (or remove the field). 4) rotate_key consults the backing store, not just cache. 5) Targeted tests where feasible.
notes: Evidence: store.c:520,360-362; store_secrets.c:92-94,309-311; audit_logger.c:150-210; policy_engine.c:27,67-72; key_store.c:386.

title: Signet: credential envelope encryption uses one store-wide DEK, not per-agent
description: README claims credential payloads are 'additionally envelope-encrypted with per-agent libsodium secretbox'. In practice store_secrets.c encrypts every credential with the single store-wide DEK from signet_store_get_dek() (store_secrets.c:53,67), using crypto_secretbox_easy with a random nonce. Encryption is real but NOT per-agent: compromise of the single DEK exposes all agents' credentials, and there is no per-agent key isolation as advertised.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Either implement per-agent key derivation (e.g. DEK subkey via crypto_kdf keyed by agent id/pubkey) for credential payloads, or correct the README/security-model to state a single store DEK. 2) If per-agent, add a test that an agent's DEK cannot decrypt another agent's payload.
notes: Evidence: store_secrets.c:53,67 store-wide DEK; README security model claims per-agent secretbox.

title: Signet: DB key handling and DEK KDF mismatch docs (no base64 decode; BLAKE2b not HKDF)
description: Two doc/impl mismatches around key handling in store.c. (1) SIGNET_DB_KEY is documented as base64-encoded 32 bytes, but signet_derive_dek (store.c:76-89) only hex-decodes 64/128-char inputs and otherwise memcpys raw ASCII bytes (cap 64). A 44-char base64 key is treated as raw ASCII, not decoded — reducing effective entropy and silently diverging from docs. (2) store.h claims the DEK is 'derived via HKDF' but the code uses keyed BLAKE2b (crypto_generichash) with domain separation (store.c:96-103). Not insecure, but the contract is wrong and interop/rederivation could break.
acceptance_criteria: 1) DB key input format is well-defined (accept base64 AND/OR hex explicitly) and decoded accordingly; reject under-entropy keys. 2) Header/README KDF description matches implementation (or switch to real HKDF). 3) Test covers key parsing for each accepted format and rejection of weak keys.
notes: Evidence: store.c:76-89 no base64 decode; store.c:96-103 BLAKE2b; store.h:6-8 says HKDF.

title: Signet: replay cache fails open on missing/malformed event id or timestamp
description: replay_cache.c returns SIGNET_REPLAY_OK (accept) instead of rejecting when: event_id is NULL/empty (replay_cache.c:159), event_id length > 256 (replay_cache.c:169), or event_created_at <= 0 (replay_cache.c:143). Any caller that fails to extract a proper id/timestamp gets NO replay protection and no skew check. Fail-open weakens the replay defense described in the README.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Missing/empty/overlong event id -> rejected (or a hard error), not accepted. 2) Invalid timestamp -> rejected or explicitly handled, not silently accepted. 3) Tests assert rejection for each malformed input.
notes: Evidence: replay_cache.c:143,159,169 return SIGNET_REPLAY_OK on bad input.
close_reason: Implemented and built; see commit. Details in each issue's fix.

title: Signet: set_policy persistence is best-effort — ACK claims success but policy lost on restart
description: policy_store.c:930 returns 0 (success) after applying a submitted policy in-memory even when durable persistence to the policy file fails ('persistence is best-effort'). The SET_POLICY management ACK then reports success, but the policy is lost on daemon restart. Operators relying on the ACK will believe the change is durable.
acceptance_criteria: 1) set_policy returns an error (or a clearly-degraded status) when persistence fails, and the mgmt ACK reflects it. 2) On success the policy is confirmed written to disk. 3) Test: force write failure and assert non-success is surfaced.

title: Eager thread prefetch: build thread graph on event arrival
description: When kind:1 or kind:1111 events arrive via the live relay stream, eagerly prefetch their root and parent events from relays so the thread panel can render instantly without waiting for on-open relay fetches. Creates a new GnostrThreadPrefetch service in apps/gnostr/src/util/ that hooks into on_multi_sub_event, parses NIP-10 tags, checks nostrdb, and batches relay queries for missing ancestors.
close_reason: Implemented GnostrThreadPrefetch service with refcounted lifecycle, debounced batch dispatch, nostrdb-first lookup, serialized relay queries, and proper owner-context source attachment. Wired into main window startup/dispose and on_multi_sub_event hook.

title: [F35-F39-F45] Test hardening: sleep-based tests, mock fidelity, missing coverage
description: MEDIUM: Test improvement batch. (F35) Replace g_usleep with deterministic helpers in test_event_flow_ingest_subscribe.c. (F36) Add relay error-path tests for callback completion, network errors, no-EOSE timeout. (F37) Fix fake_relay_fixture.py limit handling and add signature verification. (F38) Add relay-level NIP-47 integration tests with real correlation IDs. (F39) Add NIP-65 stale event and write-relay publish tests. (F45) Enable TCP auth and ACL test coverage in CI. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F35-F39, F45
notes: Phase 2: Test hardening deferred. Remaining findings: F35-F39, F45

title: [F19-F21-F24-F26-F27-F28-F29-F31-F32-F33-F34] Medium priority structural fixes
description: MEDIUM: Batch of structural improvements. (F19) CLOSED reason dropped on channel-full. (F21) Double-close of events channel. (F24) Global mutex prevents concurrent negentropy sessions. (F26) Error detection by string sniffing. (F27) relay_fetch.c nostr-as-HTTP pattern. (F28) grelay polling ticks instead of put_event fan-out. (F29) max_subs defaults to 1. (F31) policy_decider.c duplicates ring-buffer state/symbols. (F32) query_sync is blocking-only. (F33) NIP-17 pool init not thread-safe. (F34) Thread subscription filters only root ID. Full details in docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: [F22-F23] Wire NIP-11 probing and fix NIP-46 timeout infrastructure
description: MEDIUM: (F22) nip46_rpc_call stores timeout_ms but blocks forever on go_channel_receive — replace with go_select_timeout using the stored timeout value. (F23) NIP-11 relay info parsing is fully implemented but never probed before connecting/syncing — check supported_nips for NIP-77 before negentropy sync, check auth_required before subscribing. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F22, F23
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: [F18-F20-F30] Fix deduplication defaults and reconnect re-delivery
description: MEDIUM: Three related dedup/replay issues. (F18) simplepool dedup off by default, O(n) linear scan up to 65K entries — default to on, use hash set. (F20) relay_refire_subscriptions reuses original since filter, re-delivering old events on reconnect — track last_seen_created_at and advance since. (F30) Replay dedup TTL defaults to 0 (disabled) in protocol_nip01.c — default to 300s. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F18, F20, F30
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: [F16-F17-F25] Replace polling loops with event-driven patterns
description: MEDIUM: Three polling-instead-of-callbacks patterns. (F16) reconnect_now() is a no-op — add a reconnect_now channel to wake from backoff. (F17) subscription_monitor_thread uses 1ms polling loop — fix the go_select race and revert to blocking select. (F25) nip46_session connect_monitor_thread polls relay state every 200ms — use nostr_relay_set_state_callback instead (pattern already demonstrated in neg-client.c). Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F16, F17, F25
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: RefDB cache never refreshes; detached HEAD dropped; publish_state malloc failures
description: (1) No refresh vtable or periodic reload — concurrent updates invisible. (2) Detached HEAD silently dropped in publish_state (only symbolic HEAD captured). (3) publish_state increments ref_count when malloc fails for target hex string, sending NULL target to Nostr. (4) load_state_from_nostr silently absorbs network errors; constructor ignores return value. File: libhanami/src/hanami-refdb-backend.c.
close_reason: Fixed: (1) Detached HEAD now published as raw OID hex. (2) malloc failures in publish_state now skip the ref (continue) instead of sending NULL target. Remaining: cache refresh and network error silencing tracked separately.

title: State published before push in hanami_push_to_grasp
description: In hanami_push_to_grasp, step 2 publishes the NIP-34 state event before step 3 performs the git push. If the push fails, the published state advertises refs that dont exist on the server. No rollback path exists. Also: relay_urls/relay_count params in hanami_grasp_fetch are silently ignored. File: libhanami/src/hanami-grasp.c lines 569-675.
close_reason: Fixed by parallel agent dispatch

title: Tests never exercise core functionality (ODB read, Nostr publish/fetch)
description: Critical untested paths: (1) ODB read() for indexed OID triggering Blossom HTTP fetch. (2) Nostr publish/fetch happy paths — all tests check null guards or no-signer errors only. (3) RefDB write never verifies Nostr event publication. (4) dummy_sign returns input unmodified — no crypto testing. (5) Transport factory test never actually connects/fetches. (6) test_version_info hardcodes version numbers.
close_reason: Closed

title: Manual JSON parser is fragile and has buffer overflow risk
description: json_get_string uses 128-byte search buffer with no key length check. Escape handling only skips one char (breaks on unicode escapes). json_get_int calls strtoll on arbitrary values. hanami_blossom_mirror does not JSON-escape source URL. File: libhanami/src/hanami-blossom-client.c lines 183-295, 676.
close_reason: Fixed by parallel agent dispatch

title: Multiple memory leaks in hanami.c high-level APIs
description: (1) hanami_repo_open: mkdtemp temp directory never cleaned up. (2) ODB backend leaked when git_repository_odb fails. (3) nostr_ctx leaked when hanami_refdb_backend_new fails. (4) Error* leaked in ensure_relays on relay connection failure. (5) sign_event overwrites event->id without freeing old value. File: libhanami/src/hanami.c, hanami-nostr.c.
close_reason: Fixed 4 memory leaks: (1) ODB backend freed when git_repository_odb fails. (2) RefDB backend freed when git_refdb attach fails. (3) nostr_ctx freed when refdb creation fails. (4) Error* freed in ensure_relays on relay connection failure. (5) event->id freed before overwrite in sign_event.

title: Signet: standalone meson build can't link signetd (stale in-tree lib names)
description: Include paths are fixed (commit 4cfc83ae: libgo/include, nsync, nip11). Remaining: the standalone meson build (signet/meson.build) cannot LINK signetd/signetctl because its in-tree cc.find_library names are stale vs the monorepo CMake outputs — e.g. it looks for nip46/nip44 but the libs are libnostr_nip46_core.a / libnostr_nip44_core.a, and it never links libgo (liblibgo.a), libgo_fiber, nsync, libjson, nip01/nip04, or their transitive deps (secp256k1/websockets). Fix: sync the in-tree lib discovery + link list (and ordering) to the current CMake targets so 'meson compile' produces a working signetd against the ../build libraries. The passkey TEST targets already build+run standalone; this is only the full-daemon link. CMake build/ builds the daemon fine today, so this is convenience/parity, not a blocker.
close_reason: Standalone meson build now links signetd/signetctl + all tests: mirrored CMake's in-tree archive link line (correct names/order) + curl/nsync/secp256k1/websockets/flatccrt. meson compile builds the daemon; 9/9 meson tests pass.

title: [F40-F47] Low priority: logging, defaults, and polish
description: LOW: (F40) nostr_relay_publish logs full event JSON to stderr — redact or guard behind debug flag. (F41) sync_relays called on every query — only on relay-list change. (F42) Hardcoded profile indexer URLs — make configurable. (F43) NOTICE on every writable callback in verbose mode — remove from writable path. (F44) 300ms EOSE linger compounds with polling delays. (F46) No relay count cap in nip65_add_relay. (F47) NIP-42 example uses https:// not wss://. Ref: docs/audits/nostr-protocol-audit-2026-04-29.md F40-F47
close_reason: Implemented in parallel bucket remediation commit b17e2a6e

title: Thread safety issues in init/shutdown and ensure_relays
description: (1) hanami_ref_count is a plain int with no atomic/mutex protection — data race on concurrent init/shutdown. (2) ensure_relays has no mutex — two threads can both see relays_connected=false and race on calloc/write. (3) No bech32 checksum validation in npub_to_hex — corrupted npubs silently produce wrong pubkeys. Files: hanami.c, hanami-nostr.c, hanami-grasp.c.
close_reason: Fixed thread safety bugs: atomic ref count in hanami.c, mutex protection in hanami-nostr.c ensure_relays, and length validation in hanami-grasp.c npub_to_hex

value: signet passkeys: Phase 0 feasibility spike is GO. Software FIDO2/WebAuthn authenticator is viable — OpenSSL 3.x ES256/P-256 + minimal CBOR/COSE encoder produce attestation objects/assertions that python-fido2 independently verifies. Storage needs a dedicated passkey_credentials table (secrets table has UNIQUE(agent_pubkey), one row per agent). Synced creds: PSK-wrap keys (not the instance DEK), signCount=0. Spike + tests in signet/tests/phase0/.
